column27: Comments
Recruited heavy hitters early was key.

Game 1 went a lot better then game 2 point wise, used Hawkeye to give a boost to Wolverine and Spider-Man’s abilities with the latter gaining the bystanders and the former taking out the city villains.
 While hand 2 focused on a Hulk and Thing mostly green deck used to deal the damage to Mole Man to good effect, found it to be an interesting set up and look forward to the rest of the tournament.

First game I was able to KO quickly and then get a Hulk/Wolverine engine going with Thing’s rare and Hulk’s rare in the mix. Second game was a major fail with the HQ clogged with 5 recruit heroes and no ability to KO or do damage.
This one can get away from you quick with the stacking forcefields and escaped subterranean villains. Thank goodness Wolverine was in the pool. LOL

Went for a focus deck on one side which did really well with Surfer and Thing's rares. Spidey and the X-Men did better than I expected with strong draw power. A few early escapes definitely hurt me but I totally lost in the rematch! Kind of a deceptively tough setup in the late game. A very fun set up that felt well balanced ( I love burrow so much).
Fun! Felt good about my choices.

First game was so lucky and unlucky at the same time. I lost on timer on turn 21, despite buying 3 rares! Barely halfway through the Villain deck! Second was payback, with the right side double copying Berserker Rage on back to back turns to finish Moleman. Left side was a Thing recruit deck that held it down until the Wolvie and Rogue took off and finished it. Bummed by the 3 escapes, so tried to finish as quickly as possible.

Really want to rate the setup a 5, but want to see if other setups are as good or better.  I've been playing a lot of solo games, and gloomhaven has been my recent effort.  And it has been an effort.  It was awesome to get Legendary back on the table and be able to respond to the clarity of the deck-building mechanics.  Thanks for putting this league together.
We got wrecked!  Mole man has never been this tough!
it was tough hitting Mole Man since he got extra hits from the escaped Subterra villains AND the Twists

We tossed around a few ideas for how to approach this, yondu / Adam warlock for some shard stuff.  Amadeus for some tech support for the scheme.  We ultimately went with Amadeus and M and it worked pretty well.  Lots of bystanders due to the celestials helping us rescue bystanders when we'd fight a villain.  Cloning was super helpful a few times to give us some extra heroes. Overall it was a lot of fun.

One hand went cloning madness with M+ cucoos, other tried to go bystander combo with Hiroim and Ronan. With so much cards of same type gained from cloning Celestials became pushovers. Hand 1 had 43 cards total in deck after game ended.

After 5 scheme twists in a row to start this, I was certain attempt 1 was going to be a bust. All plans went out the window as I was scrambling to recruit Hulkbuster to prevent the loss. I ended up with a Hulkbuster and Ronan hand with Rictor for KO power that started setting me up to hit the MM almost every turn. My wife was building up bystanders with Hiroim and was taking all of the Demonic Bargains with Vishanti for a fun and quick game.

It truly all comes down to the shuffle. I kept the same hero choices and the 2nd attempt had way more momentum with early recruiting and transforming of heroes. Double boons from Nezarr made a huge difference too.

I don't like this Scheme very much. Very swingy with the early loss by twist #7. This happened in my first match, when I only needed the Final Blow in my 23rd turn! I had zero escapes at that the point and 22 rescued bystanders - would have been a great score.... ah well.....For the second try I substituted one of my chosen heroes and this worked fine. But I couldn't prevent one escape due to the masses of Sinister Clones at the beginning of my match.

Resubmitting as I noticed other left more in-depth comments, and I'd like to as well.  Clone sets (M + Stepford) didn't provide great recruit towards non-Clone heroes, and they can make for some really large decks due to having no KO abilities.  However, they can build up more attack early in the game than non-cloning cards due to their frequency + Tactical Formation, and they handled the Celestials well due to duplicate classes.  That allowed me to keep the city clear while I waited on Hiroim's transform card to get OP with bystander count (thank you Sinister Clones).  Pretty good set-up, I got hurt in Game 1 by not buying prison ship recruits early enough, and when Hulkbuster's 8-point card came up late, it blocked my ability to manage the count on the ship.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: The Celestials were in the set up, and they are my absolute favorite villain group so that was a real treat! I got the one that reduces mastermind threat by 1/5th, Nezarr, on the third turn. That was the only thing that let me get ahead as fast as I did. Left side had 3 Celestials downed and the right had 1. Really let me do some churning and rescuing more bystanders. The Lords of Netherworld didn't really throw too much of a roadblock for me. The cloning worked really well in my favor, and is bringing me around on liking the mechanic more. Overall, great set up! Only had time for one play so hopefully it does well!
Decided to go with M because of her 3 abilities and Dr. Voodoo for some flavor for some Artifact possibilities but it didn't happen as much as I had hoped. Overall still a fun game.

Double cloning the Cuckoos was fun, but otherwise just had a hard time ever feeling great about our decks. If Dormammu had a higher strength feel like we would have been toast. We got a good thing going on one hand with Gammenon's bystander mechanic fly-wheeling with Ronan's 4-cost. Hindsight on deck building might have leaned stronger in bystander mechanics for the 2 free-agents.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27:
On turn 5 I already had 8 Mr. Sinister Henchmen enter the city, that’s how I got the 4 escapes.
The setup in theory was bystander-based, but I couldn’t really make a use from the effects.
Pretty much the whole game I got so lucky with my hand 1 draws, so half way to the game I decided to just boost up my hand 1 as much as possible even with hand 2’s plays. I was able to hit Dormammu 3x on my last turn! (2 transforms for Hulkbuster Iron Man, a bunch of card draws, shards and burn-a-shard effects from Quasar…)

column2: Arnim Zola Provokes a Clash of Nations
column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Wowwwww. King BP and White Wolf on one side, Star-Lord and Angel Noir on the other. Only saw one Gamora card the whole time, but it didn't matter. I got an early Mind Stone which gave me enough recruit to get both King BP and White Wolf's rares. Never once had a master strike to mess up my HQ and eventually got Zola down to only 6 attack. My other side had three Star-Lord's Element Guns generating shards so it was a quick one. Lots of fun!

Zola can be frustrating, especially when King Black Panther’s rare shows up in the HQ. The Infinity Gems are a pain too, especially in tournaments where you’re having to hit high VP Villains for little to no VP benefit. I actually think Drax was the better Hero choice but my game with him got railroaded by an unbroken March of Infinity Gems leading to an escape at the start of the game. Daredevil Noir got a better shuffle and managed more VP in less time with zero escapes, so I went with that one!

Enjoyable combo. Brought in Iron Man Noir and Shuri to emphasize tech strats (though Shuri didn't show up as early as I would've liked). I was able to build strong decks and keep things confidently under control, but due to a non-ideal HQ things lasted longer than I would've liked.

Brought Gamora/Star-Lord to reduce the printed attack available to be in the HQ. Gamora was also highly supported with all the covert cards available in the randomized pool. Shards also made the scheme easier, as they provided attack that didn't get discarded on a twist. Just always kept a non-henchman villain available, and I never lost one scheme challenge!!

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: My strategy was to have all recruit in the HQ...Zola was approx. 14 hit points each turn for me becasue I had King Black Panther Rare and Angel Noir Rare that I couldn't recruit from HQ..made this scenario more difficult

Heavy RNG on this one. First time through was about golden with getting 2 space gems to farm. Absolute trash on second attempt. Wasn't expecting it but had fun with Identical Twin Brother kicking out high scratches.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Angel Noir was a nice counter to Zemo's Ultimate Abomination

Game 1 was a disaster, with 2 rares starting in the HQ, and seeing 4 the whole game making Zola impossible. 

Game 2 leaned on my picks of Cap '41 and Bucky *hard* with the other deck doing it's best to lower abomination. After making it through 5 of the 8 first Twists, I was dead when the final one showed up, but it thankfully stopped at 10 long enough for me to Man Out Of Time the crap out of Zola.
2nd Game was rough with all the early Twist
I hate this scheme lol
Valkyrie had not much synergy with the 2 other heroes, but her thrown artifact was useful and we recruited multiple copies each games. All their other cards were never recruited.

Pretty easy setup overall. The difficulty will pretty much come down to how quickly the Mandarin's Rings come out vs. how quickly the Scheme Twists come out. In both my games I only ever saw 4 Rings, which wasn't great, and in both games the Twists seemed to be largely in the top half of the deck, which wasn't great either. I also got all 5 Master Strikes early both games. Still, the City was never even close to filling, and I had a number of turns with it completely empty.

My decks were mostly a little slow to get set up, which resulted in the game taking longer than it should have (my first attempt went to 36 turns, second 28). I picked characters with lots of Undercover abilities to crank up my SHIELD Level, and that mostly worked - both players in both games got up to at least level 7. I didn't have quite as many cards as I wanted that really needed it that high though; maybe I should've picked Coulson instead of Quake. I did manage to get at least three 24+ damage turns in the second game which was fun, and fired off Quake's rare once for 14 damage all on its own.

MVP honestly might have been Mr. Fantastic, as I was lucky and had three copies of "Twisting Equations" in the Dark Betrayal pile. That card is crazy; I had several turns with 10 or even 11 cards in hand. And with all the Undercover deck-thinning, in game 2 Player 2 only had 11 cards left in their deck.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Good Round
We enjoyed this setup. Getting some powerful Mr. Fantastic cards (One Gigantic Hand) can in handy for building powerful attacks lol.
First game I got lucky with the twists, second game not so much.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Why does every Valkyrie card cost 1 more than it should? Getting Unstable Molecules off a turn 3 twist was very nice!
A fun setup with the right Mr. Fantastic cards going to the right decks. Darkhawk and Deathlock paired so well, and Sif’s winged helm helped a lot to prevent wounds from twists and strikes.
Colson + Mr Fantastic + invisible woman rare = whole deck in your hand. Fun times.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Surprisingly, the MVP of the game was Mr.Fantastic. Some lucky early scheme twists (odd statement) led to early mr fantastic draw abilities, leading to fairly consistent 7-10 card hands. Took some time to close out still but lots of fun with crazy combos.
Keeping the city controlled was hard as rings coming back into the city pushing things around.
Nice challenge for both playthroughs.

column2: Onslaught Splices Humans with Spider DNA
column27: In our first attempt, the Heroes worked out really well but we ran out of Villain Deck before we got all the tactics. The second game we got Gamora's rare for 5 Attack thanks to Random and she blasted Onslaught away. Grey (Hand 1) had Berserk and Queen (2) had Shards. Very good combos. Also, Groot and Spider-Woman and Cannonball are great counters too Onslaught's Gimpy Hand nerf.
This was TOUGH! Onslaught will now commence to give me nightmares. lol.
Oof
Our first game we weren't able to move quick enough to take out Onslaught. Then game 2 pretty much everything that could go wrong did lol. Still haven't defeated Onslaught with randomized setups
This was pretty much unwinnable. Onslaught is incredibly tough himself and the heroes were tough to synergize

tried to use Star-Lord to get around the five car penalty, but had to much trouble recruiting at the beginning by the time i got anything going it was to late. had Banshee on the other side trying to help Havok, but had the same issues. lack of k.O. opportunity hurt as well, had 4 grey in the K.O. pile at the end, technically they were on Onslaught, the one tactic we saw in two games :(

Well, it's not saying much, but that was the longest I've ever lasted against Onslaught!  The city was flooded with Sinister Six villains from the beginning, so we were just fighting the inevitable.  Onslaught's master strike is a real killer!
If I wrote how I truly felt about these two games, I would somehow manage to violate the terms of service of every service available on the internet
This setup was not fun at all. There was literally nothing we could do against the combination of the domination effect, the 1 less max hand size, and the +3 buff to sinister six villains...

First attempt was very lucky. 2nd attempt was a little slower. Evasive acrobatics into Light the way worked out beautifully. So my hero choices didn't actually help much since BW and Photon did most of the work. The other side absorbed most of the wounds with Deadpool and even hit the MM once. Player 2 mainly kept the city clear.

Good and a rather easy game to start with! Macho Gomez is much nastier when playing Advanced Solo. When I saw the given heroes I immediately knew, Angel would fit here perfectly! Deadpool for the Wounds, Photon & Angel for my "discard"-Engine, but Black Widow had no good paring yet. So I added the partners Falcon & Wintersoldier for some more Dark Memories. I'm very pleased with my selections! :)
Angel, Photon, S.H.I.E.L.D. Black Widow and Quicksilver are deadly together!

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Really KOed deck, when Player two using Light the Way and discarding card to boost the attack and kill henchmen and MM. Player one using Punisher and BW to kill the Deadpool's friend. Funny setup, but with all the KOing you never know when the top card of your deck will be non grey card. Fun setup!

SBPKECN managed to pull off an infinite with a six card deck, after attacking a Weasel in the city, grabbing Photon's uncommon from me, and using a dodge ability! That was what I was aiming for, but she was super proud to pull that off. Especially after losing the copy she purchased to a sniper rifle assassin 5 turns prior!

Hyperspeed and Dark Memories worked well together with the double cards from Falcon and Winter Soldier on the left side. Right side went BW/Photon and used Dodge to drive Photon's Light the Way for big hits. Never played this scheme before, but it sucked. Burned 5 non-grey heroes on 3 twists, didn't see enough grey heroes there.
The plan kinda worked?
Quit sniping my rares, Macho! lol
Had fun. Thanks for setting this up

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: pretty easy. hit 3 master strikes super early sadly
There’s some pretty good combos with scarlet spider and an x student sidekick.. Iykyk 🤷🏽‍♂️

Solid gameplay, I really love the flavor of mysterio but gall he just won't die! Happy with the heroes I chose, used the same ones for both games. Deck 1 was all about using the Smash Mechanic and getting big numbers. Deck deck was all about gaining shards and burning them for benefit. The hero decks were fun but the villain deck felt a bit too mish mashed and not much of a challenge.

The RNG of twists was make or break for this setup. Our first game we got slaughtered due to 3 scheme twists in the first 7 turns. Seems like a better scheme for bigger villain decks. Still fun though.
Actually surprised with how the hero’s worked together even with 7 of them.
We rank it pi out of 5
Have to hit Mysterio quick.

I had good synergy from the first run and thought I could get it complete the second time around. I spent to much time clearing the city instead of focusing on knocking down mysterio. I wish I had a couple more of the quasar ko cards or moon knight ones.

The mix of heroes was fun.

Every other aspect of that setup can take a running leap.

This scheme seemed to be a race to find the quickest way to 8 attack. Not much you can do to mitigate against the scheme outside of recruiting no attack cards early . I couldn't worry about getting a nice engine going with a synergized deck. "Hulk Smash! And Fast!" was the order of the day.

column2: Annihilus Infiltrates the Lair with Spies
column27: My first attempt went ok, but I had bad luck with the Villain Deck, and Enchantress' "Soul Sacrifice" never came out. Running it back I chose heroes based more on their colors and had much better luck with the Villain Deck. The side that got Soul Sacrifice in this game was going nuts with KO and ended up with just 5 cards in their deck lol. And speaking of sacrificed souls, Thanos threw 29 people to their doom in this game, making his rare an Utter Annihilation for Annihilus!

Decided to go with juggernaut as one of my choices as Thanks has a lot of higher cost cards and they made a great pair as did Loki and the enchantress. Fun setup the 2nd turn around…..the first attempt Annihilus just zoomed through the city

This game had great hero synergies. Thanos and Speedball took advantage of KOed bystanders. Cloak & Dagger and Storm & Black Panther took advantage of Multiclass and Divided cards. Both games were good. My turn count on the first was a bit longer, but I felt no need to change heroes.
Utter Annihilation Thanos > Annihilus

Not sure if you saw my other comment but these two were far more fun and I don't think Thanos was to blame lol. I ended up slightly worst off than what I submitted last time but not by much. I was so close to infinite electro but game 1 did end with me dodging until I have more than enough attack to clear the city including momentumed Galactus.

The first run was so smooth. Strikes revealing strikes and twists landing the turn after we bought the last spy. Took our time beating everyone foolish enough to step into the city, which included the big G twice.

Man I thought I was going to be done for when I drew Weaponized Galactus and a Command Strike in the first three turns, but then proceeded to just get henchman and bystanders until I could build up a solid hand. Super lucky
I ALWAYS love Thanos games, especially when I get to pair him with a Cabal hero. Every hero in the setup pulled their own weight really effectively. I'm thrilled to be a part of this!

First game was super fun! Don’t think we did the best but the left sides’s Sidekick deck really popped off late game, and right side built a solid mostly-ranged deck for some killer combos. Didn’t see a Rare until turn 30 and got lucky that Galactus didn’t show up until late game as well. Second playthrough was more brutal with bad starting hands, clogged HQ, and overrun city. And yet somehow we ended up with the same exact turns and escapes on both plays, just had 5 more VP on the first play. Also, dummy me forgot to count Bystanders. wompwomp

First game I played was the first time I managed to get an Enchantress engine working. Second game was the first time I got a Thanos engine working. Psi-Lord and Proxima worked amazingly together both games. Storm and Black Panther were also there,

first game went great! exactly like planned deck wise.
it felt tough but i felt like i had it in control (it got close in the end though) 
2nd game since i already racked up a win i rolled with the same hero choices to just see if i could get a better score, but hq got clogged by high cost pretty much from the start,
i crawled back to the final blow but the plot got the better of me.
